channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India tests new mobile-based disaster alert system with nationwide test alerts
item.description: On Saturday, mobile phone users across India received test notifications labelled as 'extremely severe alerts' as part of the Union Government’s initiative to trial a nationwide disaster warning syste...

item.title: Teodoro says no to peace talks with NPA after Negros Occidental incident
item.description: Defense Secretary Gilbert Teodoro has firmly rejected the possibility of resuming peace talks with the New People's Army (NPA) following a recent armed encounter in Toboso, Negros Occidental. The inci...

item.title: PM Modi Appoints Joram Aniya and R Balasubramaniam as Full-Time Members of NITI Aayog
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has approved the appointments of Joram Aniya and R Balasubramaniam as full-time members of NITI Aayog, following the recent reconstitution of the policy think tank on Apri...

item.title: Cameron Young holds strong lead at Cadillac Championship as rain looms
item.description: Cameron Young has continued his dominant performance at the Cadillac Championship, maintaining a six-shot lead heading into the final round. After a solid 2-under 70 on Saturday, he sits comfortably a...

item.title: Four injured in Brixton drive-by shooting, police appeal for witnesses
item.description: In the early hours of Saturday morning, a drive-by shooting occurred on Coldharbour Lane in Brixton, South London, leaving four people hospitalised. A 25-year-old man suffered life-threatening injurie...

item.title: The Best Jarred Butter Beans: A Taste Test and Rating
item.description: This article explores the top jarred butter beans available on the market, with a focus on their flavour, texture, and value. Beans, once considered a cheap filler, are now recognised for their rich n...

item.title: Five Amarillo pickleball players die in small plane crash near Austin, Texas
item.description: Five members of the Amarillo Pickleball Club have tragically lost their lives in a plane crash near Wimberley, Texas, while travelling to a tournament. The victims, identified as Seren Wilson, Brooke ...

item.title: Edmonton Cryptocurrency Firm Offers Input on Proposed Federal ATM Regulations
item.description: An Edmonton-based cryptocurrency company has expressed a willingness to cooperate with the federal government regarding proposed regulations targeting cryptocurrency ATMs. The proposed measures aim to...

item.title: India finish with bronze after 0-3 defeat to France in Thomas Cup semifinals
item.description: India’s campaign in the Thomas Cup Finals came to an end with a 0-3 loss to France in the semifinals, held at Horsens, Denmark. The Indian team was missing Lakshya Sen, whose absence was felt keenly. ...

item.title: BBC Accused of Failing to Act After Presenter Allegedly Assaulted Colleague
item.description: The BBC has come under scrutiny following allegations that a presenter assaulted a female colleague, breaking her wrist, in an incident reportedly occurring around 2014. Sources indicate that the broa...

item.title: US to Pull 5,000 Troops from Germany Amid NATO Tensions
item.description: The United States has announced it will withdraw approximately 5,000 troops from Germany over the next six to twelve months, in a move that has raised eyebrows among NATO allies and top Republican law...

item.title: Kwankwaso discusses legal challenges in ADC and possible consultations with NDC
item.description: Senator Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso don break silence over di matter wey dem dey talk say e fit leave African Democratic Congress (ADC). According to reports, Kwankwaso dey consider to join Nigeria Democrati...

item.title: Edo Chief Judge Accused of Ignoring NJC, Stalling 5 Billion Naira Libel Suit at Advanced Stage
item.description: There is fresh controversy in the Edo State judiciary over a protracted N5 billion libel suit between Mike Igini, a former resident electoral commissioner of INEC, and retired Colonel David Imuse, ex‑...

item.title: Reflections on the Phrase 'Business Unusual'
item.description: In this article, Fr. Raymund Benedict 'RB' Hizon, SJ, explores the meaning and implications of the phrase 'business unusual.' He highlights how unexpected circumstances often disrupt normal routines, ...

item.title: Likuyani MP Innocent Mugabe Stands by Ruto, Focuses on Constituency Development
item.description: Likuyani MP Innocent Mugabe has stood firm despite pressure from some constituents to drop President William Ruto and join the opposition. Speaking in Likuyani, Mugabe emphasised that his main priorit...

item.title: Federal Court Upholds Law, Denies Lifetime Perks to Retired Balochistan Officials
item.description: The Federal Constitutional Court (FCC) of Pakistan has dismissed a plea by the Balochistan government aimed at granting lifetime facilities to retired chief secretaries and their widows. Justice Aamer...

item.title: Clash Over Land in Takowangwa Community Leaves One Dead, Several Injured
item.description: Tension don dey rise for Takowangwa Community inside Mokwa Local Government Area, Niger State as armed men from Etisheshi Community storm the community land on April 22 and 23, 2026. According to the ...

item.title: 21 Promising European Startups Gaining Attention in AI and Tech
item.description: Europe's startup ecosystem is attracting increasing attention in the AI and technology sectors, with companies ranging from early-stage ventures to near-unicorn status. TechCrunch highlights 21 Europe...

item.title: Durham police officers face trial over fatal Highway 401 collision
item.description: Two Durham Regional Police officers are set to stand trial following a multi-vehicle crash on Highway 401 in Whitby in April 2024, which resulted in the deaths of four people, including an infant and ...

item.title: Kayole Boy Tito and Siblings to Move into New 3-Bedroom Home in Machakos
item.description: Tito Wambua, a young boy from Kayole who has been looking after his siblings due to his mother often being away, has received a big show of support from Kenyans and the government. After his story wen...
